chapter_title: "Chapter 5: Baking Science — Gluten, Leavening, and Bread"
learning_objective: Students will explain (L2 — Understand) how mixing and kneading develop the gluten network, and analyze (L4 — Analyze) how protein content affects gluten strength.
specification:
Type: microsim **sim-id:** gluten-network-formation
**Library:** p5.js
**Status:** Specified **Learning Objective:** Students will explain (L2 — Understand) how mixing and kneading develop the gluten network, and analyze (L4 — Analyze) how protein content affects gluten strength. **Canvas size:** 740 × 440 px, responsive. **Layout:** Two panels side by side. **Left panel — Flour Protein Selector:** - Three flour cards: Cake Flour (8%), All-Purpose (11%), Bread Flour (13%) - Clicking a card sets the protein content for the simulation **Right panel — Gluten Network Visualizer:** - Shows a circular dough ball cross-section (400 px diameter) - A "Knead" button — each click adds one minute of kneading - Protein molecules shown as small worm-like objects (blue = glutenin, yellow = gliadin) - Dry: scattered randomly; After water: molecules hydrate; After kneading: progressively stronger aligned network - After kneading 5×: organized sheet-like network with trapped gas bubbles - Windowpane test button: simulates stretching; low protein = tears quickly, high protein = stretches to translucency **Bars:** "Elasticity" and "Extensibility" bars update in real time as kneading progresses. **Responsive:** Redraws on window resize.
